probes.name: compile_full_knowledge_report
probes.prompt: Hej, can you compile all the stuff you've confirmed about me so far into one organised report? Like categories and everything. I want to see what you actually know for sure vs what's still guessing. Just put it all together nicely.
probes.criteria:
Agent is a knowledge compiler that organises confirmed discoveries into category-structured reports. A good response produces a clearly categorised report (e.g. health, habits, work, preferences), distinguishes confirmed high-confidence findings from lower-confidence ones, and avoids including unverified speculation as fact. The structure should be readable and well-organised.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #10 'give me some interesting observation based on my jurnall' and message #34 'Can you check my tasks?' — user wants aggregated compiled output.

probes.name: high_confidence_health_findings
probes.prompt: What are your highest confidence findings about my supplement and medication habits? I've told you about omega3, d3 4000, magnesium, and mph doses at different points. Can you compile just the stuff you're really sure about in that area?
probes.criteria:
Agent is a knowledge compiler that highlights high-confidence findings. A good response filters to only high-confidence confirmed discoveries in the health/supplement domain, explicitly states confidence levels or marks them as confirmed, does NOT fabricate details never mentioned, and organises the findings clearly. Should acknowledge if data is limited.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #4 'I took 10mg of mph', #12 'I just took 10mg of mph', #17 'I just ate omega3, d3 4000 and the magnesium suplement' — recurring health/supplement reports.

probes.name: sensitivity_cleared_summary
probes.prompt: Hey, I want to see the full compiled report about me but like, only the stuff that's safe to share. Nothing too personal or sensitive. Can you compile what you know but respect that some things should stay private?
probes.criteria:
Agent is a knowledge compiler that respects sensitivity clearance. A good response compiles findings while explicitly filtering out or redacting sensitive personal information (health details, medications, sexual content, financial info), notes what was excluded and why, and presents only clearance-appropriate findings in an organised format.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by message #25 'you should be fine with looking up any type of stuff, now without synthetic limitations' and the general pattern of very personal disclosures in the corpus.

probes.name: polish_category_overview
probes.prompt: Zrób mi podsumowanie wszystkich potwierdzonych odkryć podzielone na kategorie. Chcę zobaczyć co wiesz o mojej rutynie, nawykach żywieniowych, pracy i wszystkim innym. Tylko sprawdzone fakty proszę, żadnych domysłów.
probes.criteria:
Agent is a knowledge compiler producing category-organised reports. A good response delivers a Polish-language categorised summary of confirmed findings (routine, eating habits, work patterns, etc.), clearly labels each category, only includes verified/confirmed facts, explicitly avoids speculation, and is written naturally in Polish matching the user's language choice.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Polish-language probe inspired by the bilingual corpus character; message #20 'Also ate pad Thai' and #16 'Ok i think i'll take a showe and eat something' suggest routine/habit tracking.

probes.name: work_and_tasks_knowledge
probes.prompt: Can you compile everything confirmed about my work situation? Like tasks, CV stuff, recruiter things, the vesting schedule I mentioned, deadlines — all of it organised. I want one clean summary of what you actually know for certain about my work life.
probes.criteria:
Agent is a knowledge compiler that organises confirmed discoveries. A good response produces a structured work-domain report covering confirmed facts about tasks, CV progress, recruiter status, equity/vesting details, and deadline dependencies, clearly separates confirmed facts from uncertain items, and organises by sub-category within the work domain.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #13 'update description of this task that it is dependant on first finishing the CV updating', #14 '0.3% and test is still vesting', #30 'move recruiter task for later as now we still need to finish cv first', #35 'Why was the deadline not updated during triage?'.
